boardQuestion: Which AI-governance renewals are most likely to slip into exit without immediate intervention?
renewalNarrative: The AI governance lane is sticky enough to renew if we tighten the operator proof set.
exitRiskReality: Rollback, exception, and eval proof is older than the renewal story implies.
riskHeadline: AI-governance renewals need fresher operating proof before the next board-ready commercial review.
renewalSignal: Pipeline stays warm, but customer confidence is now asking for newer evidence of control durability.
missingProof: Fresh exception-handling export, renewed eval packet, and updated rollback outcomes
headline: AI governance renewals are recoverable, but only if proof freshness improves fast.
nextMove: Refresh the operating packet and narrow the renewal story to the highest-confidence outcomes.

boardQuestion: Which renewals are strongest enough to treat as commercial anchors?
renewalNarrative: Identity reviews, admin posture, and mobile protection remain renewal anchors.
exitRiskReality: Only one guest-access packet is aging, and sponsor continuity is still healthy.
riskHeadline: Identity remains the healthiest renewal cluster with only small freshness cleanup left.
renewalSignal: Customer and board confidence remain aligned with the underlying proof set.
remediationMoves: lock identity as reference renewal lane
headline: Identity renewals can close quickly with a small evidence refresh.
nextMove: Refresh the guest-access export and use identity as the commercial reference lane.

boardQuestion: Where are delivery and recovery proof gaps most likely to trigger commercial hesitation?
renewalNarrative: Hosting, release safety, and recovery operations are strong enough to defend renewal.
exitRiskReality: Backup, rollback, and recovery packets are still fragmented across too many surfaces.
riskHeadline: Platform renewals need escalation because the continuity proof chain is still fragmented.
renewalSignal: Sponsors want to renew, but board-visible continuity proof is not yet stitched together well enough.
missingProof: One end-to-end packet connecting hosting, rollback, backup, and recovery proof
headline: Platform renewals are winnable, but the continuity packet needs one owner fast.
nextMove: Escalate one owner to assemble the continuity packet before the next renewal checkpoint.

boardQuestion: Which renewal lane can anchor the rest of the commercial proof stack?
renewalNarrative: Security questionnaires, trust-center proof, and answer-pack reuse remain renewal-ready.
exitRiskReality: Coverage is strong, with only minor proof-index hygiene still helpful.
riskHeadline: Procurement is structurally strong enough to anchor the broader renewal stack.
renewalSignal: Renewal momentum is backed by clear proof reuse and stable commercial trust.
missingProof: Published proof-coverage index across the procurement stack
headline: Procurement should anchor the renewal proof stack.
nextMove: Publish the proof-coverage index and lock this lane as the reference renewal surface.

boardQuestion: Which renewals are too early or too weak to defend without scope changes or intentional exits?
renewalNarrative: Reporting, attribution, and release controls justify a clean commercial renewal at current pricing.
exitRiskReality: Cross-surface proof is too thin for the strength of the current commercial ask.
riskHeadline: Pricing pressure and thin proof make this renewal cluster the closest to intentional exit.
renewalSignal: Commercial pressure is high and the story still outruns the evidence bundle.
missingProof: One end-to-end packet linking reporting, release controls, and attributable outcomes
headline: Some pricing-sensitive renewals should be reframed as exit-managed rather than fully defended.
nextMove: Model an exit-safe path while rebuilding the linked revenue proof packet.

boardQuestion: Where is sponsor fragility most likely to turn into avoidable renewal loss?
renewalNarrative: GxP, specimen, CAPA, and safety surfaces already justify a stable renewal motion.
exitRiskReality: Evidence is credible, but sponsor continuity and freshness are weaker than the commercial confidence suggests.
riskHeadline: Sponsor fragility is the main threat to this regulated renewal cluster.
renewalSignal: Customers still value the surface, but sponsor turnover and older proof are slowing the save case.
missingProof: Fresh provenance notes, updated sponsor map, and one repeatable regulated packet
headline: Regulated renewals need sponsorship repair before the next cycle.
nextMove: Refresh sponsor mapping and publish one repeatable regulated packet before the next renewal checkpoint.
